revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/81479786|長草天神社]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''長草天神社'''（ながくさてんじんしゃ）は、[[Aichi Prefecture|愛知県]][[Ōbu|大府市]]にある[[Shinto shrine|神社]]である。MediaWiki
== 歴史 == 知多郡英比ノ荘の地頭、藤田民部は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に家人たちに長草の地の開墾を命じ、同時に民部の邸内に祀られていた[[Sugawara no Michizane|菅原道真]]を、同年[[June 19|6月19日]]に[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]として迎えたのが長草天神社の起源とされる。[[Meiji (era)|明治]]5年に村社、[[1907|明治40年]][[October 20|10月20日]]に神饌幣帛供進神社に列格される。MediaWiki
== 例祭 == 長草天神社の例祭は、創建された明応3年から[[1910|明治43年]]まで、[[Lunar calendar|旧暦]]の正月25日に行われていたが、現在は[[February 25|2月25日]]の直前日曜日に行われる。例祭には『[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭り』が行われる。MediaWiki
== どぶろく祭り == 長草天神社[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭りは、例祭に行われる[[Festival|祭礼]]で、大府市指定有形民俗文化財となっている<ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。MediaWiki
どぶろく祭りの歴史は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に地頭が寄進した供田の収穫米でどぶろくを醸造して神前に供え、その後例祭にて御神酒を氏子や参拝客に振舞ったのが始まりとされる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。この再開は当時の地頭名府士官、市村伝兵衛尉勝行の主唱とされている。MediaWiki
どぶろくの[[Brewing|醸造]]は、長草地区で6組に分かれている酒元組がどぶろくの醸造を輪番して、境内の酒造所で1月上旬から醸造が行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 酒の好きな空想上の動物「[[Shōjō|猩々]]」が境内を練り歩くほか、投げ餅なども行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 午後5時ごろ、翌年当番の組にどぶろく作りの道具が引き渡される『トウワタシ』の儀式が行われ、例祭は終了する。MediaWiki

* [[Central Japan Railway Company|JR東海]][[Tōkaidō Main Line|東海道本線]][[Kyōwa Station|共和駅]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''長草天神社'''（ながくさてんじんしゃ）は、[[Aichi Prefecture|愛知県]][[Ōbu|大府市]]にある[[Shinto shrine|神社]]である。
== 歴史 == 知多郡英比ノ荘の地頭、藤田民部は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に家人たちに長草の地の開墾を命じ、同時に民部の邸内に祀られていた[[Sugawara no Michizane|菅原道真]]を、同年[[June 19|6月19日]]に[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]として迎えたのが長草天神社の起源とされる。[[Meiji (era)|明治]]5年に村社、[[1907|明治40年]][[October 20|10月20日]]に神饌幣帛供進神社に列格される。
== 例祭 == 長草天神社の例祭は、創建された明応3年から[[1910|明治43年]]まで、[[Lunar calendar|旧暦]]の正月25日に行われていたが、現在は[[February 25|2月25日]]の直前日曜日に行われる。例祭には『[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭り』が行われる。
== どぶろく祭り == 長草天神社[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭りは、例祭に行われる[[Festival|祭礼]]で、大府市指定有形民俗文化財となっている<ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
どぶろく祭りの歴史は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に地頭が寄進した供田の収穫米でどぶろくを醸造して神前に供え、その後例祭にて御神酒を氏子や参拝客に振舞ったのが始まりとされる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。この再開は当時の地頭名府士官、市村伝兵衛尉勝行の主唱とされている。
どぶろくの[[Brewing|醸造]]は、長草地区で6組に分かれている酒元組がどぶろくの醸造を輪番して、境内の酒造所で1月上旬から醸造が行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 酒の好きな空想上の動物「[[Shōjō|猩々]]」が境内を練り歩くほか、投げ餅なども行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 午後5時ごろ、翌年当番の組にどぶろく作りの道具が引き渡される『トウワタシ』の儀式が行われ、例祭は終了する。

{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== 歴史 == 知多郡英比ノ荘の地頭、藤田民部は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に家人たちに長草の地の開墾を命じ、同時に民部の邸内に祀られていた[[Sugawara no Michizane|菅原道真]]を、同年[[June 19|6月19日]]に[[Ubusunagami|産土神]]として迎えたのが長草天神社の起源とされる。[[Meiji (era)|明治]]5年に村社、[[1907|明治40年]][[October 20|10月20日]]に神饌幣帛供進神社に列格される。
== 例祭 == 長草天神社の例祭は、創建された明応3年から[[1910|明治43年]]まで、[[Lunar calendar|旧暦]]の正月25日に行われていたが、現在は[[February 25|2月25日]]の直前日曜日に行われる。例祭には『[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭り』が行われる。
== どぶろく祭り == 長草天神社[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭りは、例祭に行われる[[Festival|祭礼]]で、大府市指定有形民俗文化財となっている<ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
どぶろく祭りの歴史は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に地頭が寄進した供田の収穫米でどぶろくを醸造して神前に供え、その後例祭にて御神酒を氏子や参拝客に振舞ったのが始まりとされる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。この再開は当時の地頭名府士官、市村伝兵衛尉勝行の主唱とされている。
どぶろくの[[Brewing|醸造]]は、長草地区で6組に分かれている酒元組がどぶろくの醸造を輪番して、境内の酒造所で1月上旬から醸造が行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 酒の好きな空想上の動物「[[Shōjō|猩々]]」が境内を練り歩くほか、投げ餅なども行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 午後5時ごろ、翌年当番の組にどぶろく作りの道具が引き渡される『トウワタシ』の儀式が行われ、例祭は終了する。

{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20|October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== 例祭 == 長草天神社の例祭は、創建された明応3年から[[1910|明治43年]]まで、[[Lunar calendar|旧暦]]の正月25日に行われていたが、現在は[[February 25|2月25日]]の直前日曜日に行われる。例祭には『[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭り』が行われる。
== どぶろく祭り == 長草天神社[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭りは、例祭に行われる[[Festival|祭礼]]で、大府市指定有形民俗文化財となっている<ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
どぶろく祭りの歴史は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に地頭が寄進した供田の収穫米でどぶろくを醸造して神前に供え、その後例祭にて御神酒を氏子や参拝客に振舞ったのが始まりとされる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。この再開は当時の地頭名府士官、市村伝兵衛尉勝行の主唱とされている。
どぶろくの[[Brewing|醸造]]は、長草地区で6組に分かれている酒元組がどぶろくの醸造を輪番して、境内の酒造所で1月上旬から醸造が行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 酒の好きな空想上の動物「[[Shōjō|猩々]]」が境内を練り歩くほか、投げ餅なども行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 午後5時ごろ、翌年当番の組にどぶろく作りの道具が引き渡される『トウワタシ』の儀式が行われ、例祭は終了する。

{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20|October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== どぶろく祭り == 長草天神社[[Nigori|どぶろく]]祭りは、例祭に行われる[[Festival|祭礼]]で、大府市指定有形民俗文化財となっている<ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
どぶろく祭りの歴史は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に地頭が寄進した供田の収穫米でどぶろくを醸造して神前に供え、その後例祭にて御神酒を氏子や参拝客に振舞ったのが始まりとされる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。この再開は当時の地頭名府士官、市村伝兵衛尉勝行の主唱とされている。
どぶろくの[[Brewing|醸造]]は、長草地区で6組に分かれている酒元組がどぶろくの醸造を輪番して、境内の酒造所で1月上旬から醸造が行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 酒の好きな空想上の動物「[[Shōjō|猩々]]」が境内を練り歩くほか、投げ餅なども行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 午後5時ごろ、翌年当番の組にどぶろく作りの道具が引き渡される『トウワタシ』の儀式が行われ、例祭は終了する。

{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20|October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
どぶろく祭りの歴史は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に地頭が寄進した供田の収穫米でどぶろくを醸造して神前に供え、その後例祭にて御神酒を氏子や参拝客に振舞ったのが始まりとされる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。この再開は当時の地頭名府士官、市村伝兵衛尉勝行の主唱とされている。
どぶろくの[[Brewing|醸造]]は、長草地区で6組に分かれている酒元組がどぶろくの醸造を輪番して、境内の酒造所で1月上旬から醸造が行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 酒の好きな空想上の動物「[[Shōjō|猩々]]」が境内を練り歩くほか、投げ餅なども行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 午後5時ごろ、翌年当番の組にどぶろく作りの道具が引き渡される『トウワタシ』の儀式が行われ、例祭は終了する。

{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20|October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
どぶろく祭りの歴史は、[[1494|1494年]]（[[Meiō|明応]]3年）に地頭が寄進した供田の収穫米でどぶろくを醸造して神前に供え、その後例祭にて御神酒を氏子や参拝客に振舞ったのが始まりとされる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。この再開は当時の地頭名府士官、市村伝兵衛尉勝行の主唱とされている。
どぶろくの[[Brewing|醸造]]は、長草地区で6組に分かれている酒元組がどぶろくの醸造を輪番して、境内の酒造所で1月上旬から醸造が行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 酒の好きな空想上の動物「[[Shōjō|猩々]]」が境内を練り歩くほか、投げ餅なども行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.
== Transport ==
* [[Central Japan Railway Company]] [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] [[Kyōwa Station]]

{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20|October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。この再開は当時の地頭名府士官、市村伝兵衛尉勝行の主唱とされている。
どぶろくの[[Brewing|醸造]]は、長草地区で6組に分かれている酒元組がどぶろくの醸造を輪番して、境内の酒造所で1月上旬から醸造が行われる<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.

{{Infobox Shinto shrine}}'''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20|October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、式典が終わると神官や役員が飲酒し、続いて氏子の接待となる。午後になって一般の参拝客にも振舞われる<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.

== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>（藤田民部が寄進した供田一反歩（約990[[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]））。 [[Kanbun (era)|寛文]]5年（[[1665|1665年]]）に時勢に従い醸造を休止した。 ところが、村内で伝染病や悪疫が流行した為、[[1668|1668年]]（寛文8年）にどぶろくの醸造が再開され、現在に至っている<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。例祭の当日は、午前中に神前での式典が行われ<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.

The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20|October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].

Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

'''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20|October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494|1494]] ([[Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市}}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
== Transport ==

In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). {{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
