{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].

== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].

The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). {{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.

In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.

In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

[[sn:Nagakusa Tenjin Shrine]] [[en:Nagakusa Tenjin Shrine]]

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Lunar calendar]] → [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]]
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
== Transport ==
* [[Central Japan Railway Company]] [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] [[Kyōwa Station]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links ==
* [http://www.obu-kankou.gr.jp/263/%e9%95%b7%e8%8d%89%e5%a4%a9%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be%e3%81%a9%e3%81%b6%e3%82%8d%e3%81%8f%e3%81%be%e3%81%a4%e3%82%8a 大府市観光協会・長草天神社どぶろく祭り] * [http://www.doburokumaturi.com/ 長草天神社どぶろく祭りレポート]

In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[1907|40th year of Meiji]].

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Aichi Prefecture]], [[Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[:en:1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[Meiji (era)|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[:en:1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[:en:February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

{{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[October 20]] in [[:en:1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[:en:February 25|February 25]]. The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
== Transport ==
* [[:en:Central Japan Railway Company|Central Japan Railway Company]] [[Tōkaidō Main Line]] [[:en:Kyōwa Station|Kyōwa Station]]
== References == {{Reflist}}
== External links ==
* [http://www.obu-kankou.gr.jp/263/%e9%95%b7%e8%8d%89%e5%a4%a9%e7%a5%9e%e7%a4%be%e3%81%a9%e3%81%b6%e3%82%8d%e3%81%8f%e3%81%be%e3%81%a4%e3%82%8a 大府市観光協会・長草天神社どぶろく祭り] * [http://www.doburokumaturi.com/ 長草天神社どぶろく祭りレポート]

== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[:en:February 25|February 25]]. The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[:en:Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.

The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[:en:Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[:en:Shōjō|Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11653648]] Wikidata name: Nagakusa-Tenjin-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[:en:October 20|October 20]] in [[:en:1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[:en:February 25|February 25]]. The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。

In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[:en:Shōjō|Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

== History == In [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[:en:October 20|October 20]] in [[:en:1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[:en:February 25|February 25]]. The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[:en:Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[:en:Shōjō|Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.

In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[:en:Shōjō|Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagakusa-Tenjin-sha]][[en:Nagakusa Tenjin Shrine]][[ja:長草天神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Nagakusatenjinsya 07 06 27.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Obu|WD=Q384809}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.029069|N|136.942883|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}|architecture_style={{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11653648]] Wikidata name: Nagakusa-Tenjin-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[:en:October 20|October 20]] in [[:en:1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[:en:February 25|February 25]]. The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[:en:Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[:en:Shōjō|Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[:en:Shōjō|Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Nagakusa-Tenjin-sha]][[en:Nagakusa Tenjin Shrine]][[ja:長草天神社]] {{Infobox religious building|image=File:Nagakusatenjinsya 07 06 27.jpg|prefecture={{ill|Obu|WD=Q384809}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.029069|N|136.942883|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|WD=Q382005|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}}|architecture_style={{ill|Ishi-no-ma-zukuri|WD=Q3155268}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11653648]] Wikidata name: Nagakusa-Tenjin-sha [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:23, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shrine for Tenjin}} [[File:Nagakusatenjinsya_07_06_27.jpg|thumb|right|alt=See caption|Nagakusa Tenjin shrine, 2007]] '''Nagakusa Tenjinsha''' (長草天神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Aichi Prefecture|Aichi Prefecture]], [[:en:Ōbu|Obu City]].
== History == In [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), Fujita Tamibu, a landowner of Eibinoso, Chita County, ordered his family members to cultivate the land of Nagakusa, and at the same time, [[:en:Sugawara no Michizane|Sugawara Michizane]], who was enshrined in the Tamibu residence, was received as [[Ubusunagami|San-dojin]], which is said to be the origin of Nagakusa Tenjinsha. The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[:en:October 20|October 20]] in [[:en:1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[:en:February 25|February 25]]. The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[:en:Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[:en:Shōjō|Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}

The shrine was upgraded to a village shrine in [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]]5 and to a shrine of offerings of sacred offerings on [[:en:October 20|October 20]] in [[:en:1907|40th year of Meiji]].
== Regular Festival == The annual festival of Nagakusa Tenjinsha was held on the 25th day of the first month of the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|Lunar calendar]] from Meio 3 to [[:en:1910|Meiji 43]], when the shrine was founded, but now it is held on the Sunday immediately before [[:en:February 25|February 25]]. The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] festival is held as the annual festival.
== Doburoku Festival == The [[:en:Nigori|Doburoku]] Festival at Nagakusa Tenjinsha is a [[:en:Festival|Ritual]] held as an annual festival, and is designated as a Tangible Folk Cultural Property by Obu City. <ref>{{Cite web |date= |title=長草天神社どぶろくまつり |url=http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |access-date=2013-05-29 |publisher=大府市 |archive-date=2016-03-05 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160305034539/http://www.city.obu.aichi.jp/rekimin/bunkazai/shitei_bunkazai/bunkazai_kaisetsu/s22.html |url-status=dead }}</ref><ref name="chunichi2016221">{{Cite news |last=石井宏昌 |date=2016-02-21 |title=知多半島 宝モノ語り 長草天神社のどぶろく祭り 地域住民が醸成見守る |page=朝刊 知多版 20 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref> <ref name="chunichi2021124">{{Cite news |last=栗山真寛 |date=2021-01-24 |title=あいちの民話を訪ねて 神事コロナ禍の今こそ |page=朝刊 県内版 12 |work=[[:en:中日新聞|中日新聞]] |publisher=中日新聞社}}</ref>。
The history of the doburoku festival is said to date back to [[:en:1494|1494]] ([[:en:Meiō|Meiō]]3), when doburoku was brewed from rice donated by the head of the land and offered to the gods, and the sacred sake was then served at an annual festival to the shrine parishioners and visitors.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>(about 990 [[:en:Square metre|m<sup>2</sup>]]) donated by Fujita Tamibu. In [[:en:Kanbun (era)|Kanbun]]5 ([[:en:1665|1665]]), brewing was suspended in accordance with the times. However, due to an epidemic and epidemic disease in the village, brewing of doburoku resumed in [[:en:1668|1668]] (Kanbun 8), and has continued to the present day.<ref name="chunichi2016221" /><ref name="chunichi2021124"/>。This resumption is said to have been spearheaded by Denbei Ichimura Katsuyuki, an officer of the Jicho Meifu at the time.
Brewing of doburoku [[:en:Brewing|Brewing]] takes place at a brewery on the temple grounds from early January, with the doburoku brewing rotated among the six groups of sake brewers in the Nagakusa area.<ref name="chunichi2016221"/>。After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the [[:en:Ujiko|Ujiko]] (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />、After the ceremony, the priests and officials drink, followed by the entertainment of the Ujiko (shrine parishioners). In the afternoon, the general public is also served.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 [[:en:Shōjō|Shōjō]], an imaginary animal that likes to drink, is paraded through the grounds of the shrine, and rice cakes are thrown into the air.<ref name="chunichi2016221" />。 Around 5:00 p.m., the "towatashi" ceremony, in which the doburoku making tools are handed over to the next year's group, concludes the annual festival.{{Citation needed|date=June 2022}}
